title: Agent Control Contract — Kubernetes Downward-API Env Convention
description: The environment-variable convention of the Agent Control Contract: the Kubernetes downward-API identity vars, drain-vs-grace hint, sharding vars, and per-endpoint intelligence credentials. Authority: agentd RFC 0014 §6.4 (the downward-API env table), RFC 0015 §6 (identity from downward API, env-only), RFC 0011 §3.3/§10 (drain-vs-grace, AGENTD_POD_GRACE_SECONDS), RFC 0019 §4.2 (sharding K/N). Reference source of truth: crates/agentd/src/identity.rs (from_env, var() empty=>unset), config.rs (lifecycle env), cluster/shard.rs (AGENTD_SHARD parse + label), intel/endpoints.rs (TOKEN_ENV per-endpoint). Read from env ONLY (valueFrom.fieldRef); the agent NEVER calls the kube API. ALL vars are OPTIONAL; absence is never a config error. Identity is DESCRIPTIVE, never load-bearing (no placement/scheduling decision). Secrets travel ONLY the *_TOKEN[_FILE] env-secret path; the config file never carries a credential.

x-debranding.rule: Neutral token is canonical; the reference-impl spelling is the CURRENT ALIAS, accepted until a GA cutover decision. Both MUST be accepted by a consumer until GA — agents in the field emit the branded AGENTD_* forms today. Branded spellings are NOT dropped.

identity.description: Identity block — Identity{run_id,instance,uid,namespace,node} (identity.rs from_env). An empty value is coerced to UNSET (var() filter), not Some(""). run_id is always present (minted by config when AGENT_RUN_ID is unset).

AGENT_RUN_ID.source: minted ULID if unset; stable across a retried Job
AGENT_RUN_ID.note: always present; the ONE identity field that is never absent

AGENT_POD_GRACE_SECONDS.provisional: NOT read in current source (config.rs has no getenv for it; only comments reference 'pod grace'). RFC 0011 §10 flags the exact name as an unsettled doc convention. Included, marked provisional/may-be-absent-in-impl.

AGENT_DRAIN_TIMEOUT.note: drain budget; also a --drain-timeout flag; lifecycle (config.rs)

sharding.description: Sharding (RFC 0019 §4.2). agentctl owns K via the StatefulSet ordinal; the agent does NOT discover its own shard.

AGENT_SHARD.validation: shard.rs parse() rejects N==0 / K>=N => EXIT_USAGE(2)
AGENT_SHARD.provisional: P3 DEFECT — a literal "K/N" cannot be produced per-pod from one StatefulSet pod template (the downward API cannot compute it). The proposed fix --shard auto/N (derive K from the AGENT_POD_NAME ordinal) is UNBUILT; current source only parses an explicit K/N. Recorded as the current token + the defect.

AGENT_SHARD_TIMER.note: timer-route shard behaviour (shard.rs TimerShard::parse); shard0 => only K==0 (or N==1) fires; keyed => every replica fires

AGENT_STANDBY.note: standby surface toggle (config.rs); maps to surfaces.standby

credentials.description: Per-endpoint intelligence credentials (intel/endpoints.rs TOKEN_ENV, RFC 0014 §6.4). 1-indexed by endpoint list position; endpoint 1 uses the unsuffixed form. Each has a _FILE variant (mounted-secret path, rotation-friendly). NEVER logged; the reference Secret newtype has no Serialize (RFC 0012 §3.7). Credentials use ONLY this env-secret path — never the identity downward-API path and never the config file.

lifecycle.description: Other control/lifecycle env (config.rs). Restart-only (env/flag, never a config-file key).

AGENT_SERVE_MCP.note: management transport address; serve_mcp:Option<String>; drives surfaces.management

AGENT_MODEL.note: operator-declared model; never a secret; reloadable via config file too

rules.description: Frozen rules of the env convention.
rules.all_optional: Every var is optional; absent => the field is None/null; never a config error (identity.rs).
rules.identity_descriptive: Identity is DESCRIPTIVE, never load-bearing — no placement/scheduling decision is made from it.
rules.empty_is_unset: An empty env value is coerced to UNSET (Some("") is never produced).
rules.no_kube_api: All values arrive via env (valueFrom.fieldRef); the agent NEVER calls the Kubernetes API.
rules.secrets_path: NO secrets via the identity downward-API path; credentials use the *_TOKEN[_FILE] env-secret path ONLY.
rules.config_file_no_creds: The config file NEVER carries a credential (RFC 0011/0012); only structural {{secret:NAME}}/{{secret-file:PATH}} references.

x-sum-types.identity_fields: Each identity env field is string-or-absent; an empty string coerces to UNSET (defeats naive presence checks).
x-sum-types.AGENT_SHARD: string "K/N" => surfaces.shard is string|null (null when N==1).
x-sum-types.AGENT_SERVE_MCP: string (transport addr) | absent => surfaces.management is false | string.

x-provisional.p_grace: AGENT_POD_GRACE_SECONDS is RFC-specified but not read in current source; name unsettled (RFC 0011 §10).
x-provisional.p3_shard: AGENT_SHARD "K/N" literal is unimplementable from one StatefulSet pod template; --shard auto/N fix is unbuilt.
x-provisional.debranding_cutover: Neutral AGENT_* tokens are canonical; branded AGENTD_* spellings are aliases accepted until a GA cutover decision; branded forms are NOT dropped.
